parameters.content:
## Competitor Content Gap Analyzer — GPT-4o-mini + Slack

For SEO teams, content strategists, and agency analysts who want to instantly compare any two web pages and get a detailed content gap report in Slack. Submit your page URL, a competitor page URL, and a target keyword via the form. The workflow scrapes both pages in parallel, strips all HTML leaving only readable text, and passes both to GPT-4o-mini which produces a 6-section analysis covering keyword usage, missing topics, your competitive advantages, content depth, five priority actions, and a quick verdict. The full report is posted directly to your Slack channel.

## How it works
- **1. Form — Submit Page URLs** collects your URL, competitor URL, target keyword, and business name
- **2. Set — Extract Form Fields** maps inputs to clean variables and adds a run timestamp
- **3–4. HTTP nodes** scrape both pages simultaneously in parallel
- **5–6. Code nodes** strip scripts, styles, and HTML tags — keeping only readable text limited to 8000 characters
- **7. Merge — Combine Both Pages** joins both cleaned pages into one pipeline
- **8. Code — Combine Page Data** safely merges both items into one clean object
- **9. AI Agent — Gap Analyzer** uses GPT-4o-mini to produce a 6-section content gap report
- **11. Set — Prepare Slack Message** assembles all report fields
- **12. Slack — Send Gap Report** posts the full analysis to your channel

## Set up steps
1. In **10. OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** — connect your OpenAI API credential
2. In **12. Slack — Send Gap Report** — connect your Slack OAuth2 credential and set your channel name
3. Activate the workflow and copy the Form URL from node 1
4. Open the Form URL in a browser, fill in all four fields, and submit

nodes.name: Section — Form Input and Field Extraction

parameters.content:
## Form Input and Field Extraction
User submits both page URLs, the target keyword, and business name. All inputs are mapped to clean variables with a run timestamp added automatically.

nodes.name: Section — Parallel Page Scraping and Cleaning

parameters.content:
## Parallel Page Scraping and HTML Cleaning
Both pages are scraped simultaneously. Each Code node strips scripts, styles, and all HTML tags — keeping only readable text limited to 8000 characters for GPT token efficiency.

## Data Merge
Both cleaned page texts are merged into a single pipeline item. A Code node safely combines them with fallback handling if either page failed to scrape.

## AI Content Gap Analysis
GPT-4o-mini compares both pages and produces a 6-section report: keyword usage, missing topics, your advantages, content depth, five priority actions, and a quick verdict.

## Slack Report Delivery
Assembles the full report message with business name, keyword, URLs, and AI analysis. Posts the complete 6-section gap report to your Slack channel.

nodes.name: Note — Bot-Protected Sites May Block Scraping

parameters.content:
## ⚠️ Bot-Protected Sites May Return 403
Some websites block automated HTTP requests and return a 403 Forbidden error. If scraping fails, add a User-Agent header to this node: Name = User-Agent, Value = Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; n8n-bot/1.0). Do the same for node 4.

parameters.formDescription: Enter your page URL and a competitor's page URL. AI will scrape both, compare them, and send a full content gap report to your Slack channel.

parameters.text:
=You are a senior SEO content strategist.

Your job is to compare two web pages targeting the same keyword and produce a detailed content gap analysis.

BUSINESS: {{ $json.businessName }}
TARGET KEYWORD: {{ $json.targetKeyword }}
YOUR PAGE URL: {{ $json.yourUrl }}
COMPETITOR PAGE URL: {{ $json.competitorUrl }}

YOUR PAGE CONTENT:
{{ $json.yourPageText }}

COMPETITOR PAGE CONTENT:
{{ $json.competitorPageText }}

Analyze both pages carefully and produce the following report. Use plain text only. No markdown. No asterisks. Use CAPS for all section headings.

SECTION 1 — KEYWORD USAGE COMPARISON
For the target keyword and related terms, compare how each page uses them. Note which page uses the keyword more naturally, in headings, in opening paragraphs, and throughout the body.

SECTION 2 — CONTENT TOPICS YOUR PAGE IS MISSING
List the specific topics, subtopics, questions, or angles the competitor covers that your page does not. Number each gap. Be specific — not generic.

SECTION 3 — CONTENT TOPICS YOU HAVE THAT COMPETITOR LACKS
List what your page covers that the competitor does not. This is your competitive advantage. Number each point.

SECTION 4 — CONTENT DEPTH AND QUALITY COMPARISON
Compare word count estimate, use of examples, use of data or stats, use of FAQs, clarity of value proposition, and calls to action.

SECTION 5 — TOP 5 PRIORITY ACTIONS
List exactly 5 specific actions to improve your page based on the gap analysis. Each action must be concrete and actionable. Number them 1 to 5. Prioritize highest impact first.

SECTION 6 — QUICK VERDICT
In 3 sentences or less: is your page stronger or weaker than the competitor for this keyword, and what is the single most important thing to fix first.

RULES:
- Plain text only. No markdown symbols.
- Be specific and data-driven. Reference actual content from both pages.
- Do not write generic SEO advice. Only insights from these two specific pages.

assignments.name: gapReport
assignments.value: ={{ $json.output || 'Gap analysis could not be generated. Please check OpenAI credentials.' }}
